summary:
• Indian companies' surging imports from Venezuelan oil fields signal growing Western Hemisphere supply diversification, potentially offsetting near-term production constraints from Venezuela's debt restructuring challenges and geopolitical isolation.

• Middle East supply route vulnerabilities are intensifying regional risk premiums, with terminal attacks in Oman and escalating Iran nuclear concerns creating spillover pressure on Western Hemisphere crude competitiveness and pricing.

• Enhanced recovery projects in North Dakota and expanded production from the Brazil-Guyana-Venezuela corridor present offsetting supply dynamics, with South American export growth competing against U.S. onshore production recovery to reshape regional market share.
